statement.markdown: Implement a function to compute the diameter of a tree. The diameter of a tree is the longest path between two vertices.
statement.inputFormat: A binary tree root node (represented as a dictionary with 'value' and 'children' keys in Python or a TreeNode structure in other languages).
statement.notes: No cycles are present in the tree.

examples.explanation: Diameter of the tree is 2 (1 -> 2 or 1 -> 3)

hints.text: Consider using DFS to traverse the tree.
Think about how to update the diameter of a node.

editorial.title: Correct Approach to Tree Diameter
editorial.intuition: Use DFS to traverse the tree and keep track of the maximum depth of the left and right subtrees of each node.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach is to compute the distance between every possible pair of nodes, which takes O(n^2) time.
editorial.betterSolution: DFS can solve this problem with O(n) time complexity by keeping track of the maximum depth of the left and right subtrees of each node.
editorial.optimalSolution: Update the diameter of a node as the maximum of the diameter of its left child, the right child, or the sum of the depth of the left child and the right child.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: This method correctly computes the diameter because it considers all possible paths in the tree.
editorial.dryRun: An example dry run to illustrate how to use the optimal solution.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update the diameter of a node

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(TreeNode* root) { // TODO: implement } };
starterCode.java: class Solution { public int solve(TreeNode root) { // TODO: implement } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, root: TreeNode) -> int: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(TreeNode* root) { int ans = 0; if (root) { ans += max(solve(root->left), solve(root->right)) + 1; } return ans; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public int solve(TreeNode root) { int ans = 0; if (root) { ans += Math.max(solve(root.left), solve(root.right)) + 1; } return ans; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, root: TreeNode) -> int: if root: return 1 + max(self.solve(root.left), self.solve(root.right))
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(root) { if (root) { return 1 + Math.max(solve(root.left), solve(root.right)); } }

solutions.python: class Solution: def solve(self, root: TreeNode) -> int: if root: return 1 + max((self.solve if child else 0 for child in root.children), default=0)
solutions.cpp: class Solution { public: int solve(TreeNode* root) { if (root) { int ans = 1; for (auto child : root->children) { ans = std::max(ans, 1 + solve(child)); } return ans; } return 0; } };
